assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Extended provides 'up to 24 months of support (vs 14 months for Regular/Stable)' in the overview table and throughout the document

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Additional cost per cluster' in the comparison table and discusses cost considerations in multiple sections, advising to 'contact your account team for pricing'

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains 'Extended doesn't get versions later than Regular — it gets the same versions but can stay on them longer before being forced to upgrade'

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: Response lists 'Delayed features: Miss new K8s features and GKE improvements for longer' and 'Technical debt: Larger version jumps when you do upgrade (1.29 → 1.32+)' as specific drawbacks

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.29) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response includes verification steps with gcloud commands to check current version and explicitly notes '1.29 is eligible for Extended channel' and mentions the '1.27+ only' minimum requirement

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'compliance-sensitive workloads', 'slower enterprise change management', and describes it as 'particularly valuable' for staying on proven versions with predictable upgrade experience

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response shows support timeline ending at 'Q2 2026' and includes 'Set calendar reminders for eventual EoS (~Q2 2026)' in the checklist, implying eventual required upgrade
